plan_adherence_notes: Both plan steps completed. All 4 design decisions (d1-d4) followed: TZ=UTC pinned, @/ imports, no mocks, NaN/negative edge cases tested. Minor planned deviation: structural assertions used for locale-dependent functions instead of exact .toBe(), as the plan's step 2 anticipated. 26 tests across all 6 exported functions, all passing.
missed_questions: No question about formatCurrency boundary behavior at the 1000 threshold (small vs K-suffix), though this is partially mitigated by format.test.ts already covering formatCurrency directly
No question about whether buildLinePath should handle non-finite coordinates (NaN, Infinity) — a minor edge case gap
reason: Test file is well-structured, covers all 6 exported functions with 26 tests, follows repo conventions (@/ imports, describe blocks, no mocks for pure functions). Edge cases are well-tested (NaN/negative for getOutcomeColor, zero-width domain for scaleNumber, empty/single-point arrays for buildLinePath, composed behavior for formatSignedCurrency). Locale-dependent tests use structural assertions which is the correct approach for CI portability. All tests pass. The research-to-plan-to-execution pipeline was coherent and the implementation matches what was planned.
